# Test fixtures: trimmed DISA XCCDF selinux STIG extracts

Each `<product>_selinux_controls.xml` is a trimmed slice of the real, official,
publicly-releasable DISA STIG XCCDF benchmark for that product, containing the
5 SELinux STIG `<Group>`/`<Rule>` families this crate maps
(`ControlFamily::{Enforcing,PolicyType,Policycoreutils,PolicycoreutilsPython,
FaillockDirContext}` in `rulesteward-selinux`'s `stig.rs`) plus 2 decoy Groups
per file (Standard Mandatory DoD Notice and Consent Banner controls -
unrelated to SELinux) so a future selector's exclusion logic has real,
non-synthetic negative fixtures. `check-content` and `fixtext` text is copied
VERBATIM from the source benchmark (byte-for-byte after entity decoding);
`description`/`ident` elements are stripped (a future parser would not need
them). `<reference>` elements ARE kept (unlike `tools/auditd-stig-update`'s
fixtures) since they use the `dc:` prefix and this crate's Benchmark root
declares `xmlns:dc="http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/"` for that reason (see
`grounding/xccdf/extract-summary.json`'s fixture-inventory caveat).

DEV-ONLY: these fixtures exist to make `tools/selinux-stig-update`'s tests (and
a future `selinux-stig-check` CI gate) run OFFLINE, without depending on the
DISA CDN being reachable during a PR. They are not shipped in the
`rulesteward` release binary or its distribution artifacts.

## Provenance

| Product | Source benchmark | Selected requirements | Decoys |
|---|---|---:|---:|
| rhel8  | RHEL 8 STIG V2R4 (02 Jul 2025), `U_RHEL_8_V2R4_STIG.zip`  | 5 | 2 |
| rhel9  | RHEL 9 STIG V2R7 (05 Jan 2026), `U_RHEL_9_V2R7_STIG.zip`  | 5 | 2 |
| rhel10 | RHEL 10 STIG V1R1 (26 Feb 2026), `U_RHEL_10_V1R1_STIG.zip` | 5 | 2 |

These are the SAME three DISA zip filenames `tools/{sshd,auditd}-stig-update`
already pin (the sshd/auditd/selinux baselines are drawn from the same three
benchmark documents). Extraction pins per session 9d's
`grounding/g7-g8-xccdf-vnumbers.md` (the same extraction that grounded
`rulesteward-selinux`'s `stig.rs` table): the exact V-numbers selected are
V-230240/V-230282/V-230241/V-250315/V-250316 (rhel8),
V-258078/V-258079/V-258081/V-258082/V-258080 (rhel9), and
V-281251/V-281249/V-280966/V-280967/V-281252 (rhel10).

The 2 decoy Groups per file are real DISA Groups that were NOT selected: the
Standard Mandatory DoD Notice and Consent Banner controls (unrelated to
SELinux). Their presence proves a future selector's exclusion logic actually
filters something, rather than passing every Group through.

## Regenerating

These files were generated by a one-off script against the full unzipped
XCCDF trees cached at
`/mnt/side-projects/9d-v0_8-wave2b/grounding/xccdf/U_RHEL_{8,9,10}_*` (session
working directory; not part of this repo). If the pinned DISA revision bumps,
regenerate by re-extracting the same Group ids from the new benchmark and
reviewing the diff before committing - do not hand-edit these files.
